description: Capability set for the dedicated Messages window (US-009). The shell self-fetches its data via app-registered Tauri commands authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers in this ACL — only plugins are), so no allow-* tokens are added for them. The window invokes: messages_window_ready (ready-handshake show/focus), open_messages_window (focus from within), list_contacts, list_company_members, get_unread_summary, plus the shared messaging commands fetch_dm_thread + send_dm (the conversation pane reuses the DM thread + composer) and send_dm_to_email (US-010 New Message compose — POSTs /v1/notify/dm by email or personUid and returns the delivered vs connection-requested discriminant), and the connection-request commands list_dm_requests + respond_dm_request (US-011 Requests segment — list_dm_requests reads GET /v1/notify/connections/requests for the pending-request cards, respond_dm_request POSTs /v1/notify/connections/{accept|decline|block} with {pairKey} and emits dm:request-update on success). The compose recipient picker (RecipientPicker.svelte) additionally invokes meetings_list_memberships (to enumerate the caller's companies for per-company member groups). All HTTP to /v1/notify/* happens in Rust, not the webview, so no http: scope is needed here. Capability drift (a command registered in main.rs but missing from this window's capability file) is the #1 failure mode — every command callable from this window must remain listed in the description above and authorized by core:default below. Mirrors desktop-alt.json / dm-detail.json.
